concept: Ecological Community / Co-Occurring Populations in Relation
description: Two or more populations of different species or source-defined living groups considered together within a selected place and time because their presence or interactions matter to an ecological question. The word does not assert harmony, mutual benefit, complete inclusion, stable membership, a food web, or a human social community. The chosen organisms, boundary, relations, and omissions remain part of the analysis.
sound_symbolism: Living 'lima' opens into mutual 'weso', letting several populations meet without merging their differences. The rounded ending suggests coexistence while leaving competition, predation, and absence equally speakable.
grammatical_notes: A countable noun: 'ha limaweso lo limaloni lorea.' (This ecological community connects populations.) State the selected place, time, populations, and relation where ambiguity matters. Distinct from 'sila' (human community), 'phaliso' (network), 'limatero' (ecosystem), and a simple list of species.

pillars.solarpunk-values: The word makes multispecies consequences available to community decisions without assuming that ecological relations are peaceful or self-balancing.
pillars.art-nouveau-aesthetics: Different living forms are understood through their patterned coexistence, while aesthetic interweaving remains distinct from ecological function.

tags.nature: several living populations considered together in ecological relation
tags.spatial: co-occurrence within a selected place or distributed area
